meta_title: Impact Windows in Lantana, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Lantana, FL. Protect your home with ESW & CWI products. Licensed, A+ BBB. Financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Windows in Lantana, FL — Coastal Protection Built for Your Home
intro:
Lantana, Florida sits just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coastline, placing every home in ZIP code 33462 squarely in the path of whatever the hurricane season brings. Whether you live in the quiet streets of Pine Ridge, the waterfront-adjacent corridors of South Shores, or the established neighborhoods of Lantana Homes, your property faces serious wind-load pressures that standard windows simply cannot withstand. With a 170 mph design wind speed designation and a documented history of eight named storms since 1990, Lantana isn't a place where homeowners can afford to cut corners on hurricane protection.

The stakes are real when you consider that the average home value in Lantana hovers around $350,000 — a substantial investment that deserves professional-grade protection. Approximately 55% of homes in the area were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping improvements introduced to the Florida Building Code following Hurricane Andrew. Many of those older homes in Lantana Homes and Pine Ridge were constructed with single-pane or standard double-pane windows that offer virtually no meaningful resistance to wind-driven debris or the pressure fluctuations created during a major storm. Upgrading to impact-resistant windows isn't a luxury for these homeowners — it's a critical structural necessity.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has spent years helping Lantana homeowners make this transition with confidence. As 'The Kings of Service,' our team understands the unique environment that comes with living close to the Intracoastal Waterway and the Atlantic Ocean — the salt air, the humidity, the afternoon storm cells that build fast over the water, and the full-force hurricane threats that have visited this part of Palm Beach County multiple times in the last two decades. We bring 60+ years of combined installation experience, 100% in-house crews, and a deep familiarity with local permitting requirements through the Town of Lantana Building Department. From your first consultation near Lantana Beach to the final inspection sign-off, we handle every detail so you don't have to.

section_why.h2: Why Lantana Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you're evaluating impact window companies for your Lantana home, the differences that matter most aren't always visible in a sales brochure. They show up on installation day — in how cleanly a frame is set, how precisely a seal is applied, and how correctly a product is anchored to meet the structural demands required by Palm Beach County's building codes. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we perform every installation with our own in-house crew. We never subcontract. That means the same trained, experienced professionals who quoted your job are the ones showing up at your front door in Pine Ridge or South Shores to do the work.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience to every project — experience earned across hundreds of homes throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We've completed more than 500 homes and carry an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, which reflects not just the quality of our installations but the integrity of how we operate. We're fully licensed and insured, and we maintain relationships with the Town of Lantana Building Department to ensure every permit is properly filed and every inspection passes the first time. For homeowners near landmarks like Sportsman's Park or the Old Key Lime House, that local knowledge translates directly into a smoother, faster project.

We also understand that choosing impact windows is a major financial decision for most families in Lantana. Our team takes a consultative approach — assessing your home's existing window openings, discussing your priorities (storm protection, energy efficiency, noise reduction), and recommending the right product line for your specific situation. Whether you're in an older pre-1994 ranch home in Lantana Homes or a newer construction property closer to the Boynton Beach border, we size and specify every window to match your home's architecture and your budget. No overselling, no unnecessary upgrades — just honest advice backed by real expertise.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing stands behind its work long after installation day. Our reputation in the South Florida market is built on referrals from satisfied homeowners, and that means we're invested in your satisfaction for the long haul. When neighbors in Lake Worth Beach or Hypoluxo ask who installed your windows, we want you to recommend us without hesitation. That accountability is built into everything we do.

section_history.h2: Lantana's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Lantana's storm résumé over the past two decades is a sobering reminder of why impact-resistant windows are essential in this part of Palm Beach County. Hurricane Frances struck in September 2004, bringing sustained winds and prolonged exposure to wind-driven rain that exposed the vulnerabilities in older window systems throughout the region. Many homes in South Shores and Lantana Homes sustained significant water intrusion through standard window frames that simply weren't designed to hold up under those conditions. Less than 14 months later, Hurricane Wilma roared through in October 2005 — and this time, the damage was even more widespread. Wilma's forward speed and peak gusts left a trail of broken windows, torn shutters, and interior damage across coastal Palm Beach County communities, including Lantana.

More recently, Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as another wake-up call. While the storm's core tracked up Florida's west coast, its eastern bands brought meaningful wind speeds and debris activity to Lantana. Homeowners who had already upgraded to impact windows largely rode out the storm without incident, while those still relying on accordion shutters or plywood discovered just how stressful — and vulnerable — that experience can be. With eight named storms affecting the Lantana area since 1990, the statistical case for permanent, built-in protection is impossible to ignore.

These storms have fundamentally shaped how Lantana residents think about home protection. Today's impact window technology is specifically engineered to withstand the pressure cycles, flying debris, and sustained wind loads generated by major hurricanes — not just to survive them, but to keep your home's envelope intact so that interior damage is minimized. For a community this close to the Atlantic, that kind of reliability isn't optional — it's the standard.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Lantana
section_code.body:
Lantana falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction for building code compliance, and while it sits outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that covers Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas, the Florida Building Code still imposes stringent requirements for hurricane-rated products throughout the county. All replacement windows in Lantana must meet the design wind speed requirement of 170 mph, meaning every product installed must carry appropriate Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval (FPA) documentation demonstrating it has been tested and rated to handle those loads. This is not a suggestion — it's a legal requirement enforced at the permit level.

All impact window installations in Lantana require a permit through the Town of Lantana Building Department. This process involves submitting product approval documentation, engineered drawings for the window openings, and proof of contractor licensure before any work begins. The Town then schedules inspections at key stages of the installation to verify that the work meets code. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — we prepare and submit all necessary documentation, coordinate inspection scheduling, and ensure that your installation passes without issue. Homeowners who hire unlicensed contractors or skip the permit process expose themselves to significant legal and financial risk, including issues with insurance claims and property resale.

For older homes — particularly the 55% of Lantana properties built before 1994 — code compliance during a window replacement project may also trigger requirements to upgrade surrounding rough openings or structural elements to meet current standards. Our team evaluates these conditions during the pre-installation assessment, so there are no surprises once work begins. Understanding the code landscape in Lantana before the first window is removed is part of what separates Premier Impact Windows & Roofing from less experienced contractors.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Products for Lantana's Coastal Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted product lines in the South Florida impact window market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for the demanding wind and moisture conditions found in coastal communities like Lantana, and both carry the product approvals required for installation in Palm Beach County. These aren't off-the-shelf window brands — they're purpose-built hurricane protection systems designed to perform when it matters most.

ESW impact windows feature laminated glass constructed with a shatter-resistant interlayer that holds the glass intact even when struck by high-velocity debris. This is the same technology used in automotive windshields, but engineered to meet the far more rigorous demands of hurricane-force wind loads. ESW's multi-chamber frame profiles provide structural rigidity while minimizing thermal transfer — a critical feature for Lantana homeowners dealing with intense coastal sun and high humidity year-round. The Low-E glass coatings available on ESW windows significantly reduce solar heat gain, which translates directly into lower cooling costs during Florida's long summer season.

CWI windows bring a complementary product philosophy with robust aluminum framing systems and a wide range of configurations including single-hung, double-hung, casement, and picture window options. CWI's product lineup is particularly well-suited for the architectural variety found in Lantana's neighborhoods — from older ranch-style homes in Pine Ridge to more contemporary builds near South Shores. Both ESW and CWI products are rated to handle Lantana's 170 mph design wind speed requirement, and both come with manufacturer warranties that protect your investment well beyond the installation date. Our team will help you determine which product line best fits your home's specific window openings, architectural style, and performance priorities.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in Lantana — and How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
For a home valued at approximately $350,000 in Lantana, a full impact window replacement project typically represents an investment in the range of $8,000 to $20,000 or more, depending on the number of openings, window sizes, configuration types, and product tier selected. Single-story homes in Lantana Homes or Pine Ridge with a standard number of windows will generally land in the lower-to-middle portion of that range, while larger or multi-story properties with custom configurations may fall toward the higher end. These figures reflect professional-grade, code-compliant installation with permitted work — not the discounted quotes sometimes offered by unlicensed operators who cut corners on materials or skip the permit process.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options that require $0 down and start as low as $87 per month. Our financing programs are designed for middle-income homeowners who want to move forward with a critical home improvement without depleting savings or waiting years to accumulate cash. When you factor in the energy savings from Low-E glass coatings — which can meaningfully reduce cooling bills in a coastal Florida home — and the insurance premium reductions that typically follow a wind mitigation inspection, the net monthly cost of your impact windows is often significantly lower than the financing payment alone.

From a pure return-on-investment perspective, impact windows are one of the highest-value improvements a Lantana homeowner can make. National and regional real estate data consistently show that impact windows increase resale value, and in a Palm Beach County market where buyers expect hurricane protection, homes with fully installed impact windows tend to sell faster and at stronger prices. For a $350,000 home, even a 3–5% value increase represents $10,500–$17,500 in added equity — which means your windows can essentially pay for themselves over time.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Lantana Homeowners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to upgrade to impact windows in Lantana is the effect on your homeowner's insurance premium. Florida insurance carriers are required to offer discounts for homes that meet wind mitigation standards, and impact windows are one of the most effective qualifying improvements a homeowner can make. After your new windows are installed, you can schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector — the inspector documents your window ratings and opening protection, and that report is submitted to your insurance carrier to trigger the applicable credits.

For Lantana homeowners, the savings can be substantial. Depending on your insurer and your home's overall wind mitigation profile, impact windows alone can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 20–45%. In a coastal Palm Beach County market where insurance rates have increased sharply in recent years, that reduction can translate to hundreds of dollars per year in savings. Common carriers operating in the Lantana market — including Citizens Property Insurance and several private admitted carriers — all have wind mitigation discount structures that reward impact window upgrades. Over a 10-year period, cumulative premium savings can offset a meaningful portion of the original installation cost.

It's also worth noting that impact windows eliminate the need to purchase, store, and deploy storm shutters — a hidden cost and logistical burden that many Lantana homeowners underestimate. Accordion shutters require maintenance and eventual replacement. Plywood boarding is time-consuming, labor-intensive, and often unavailable when a storm is approaching on short notice. Impact windows provide always-on protection with zero deployment required, which means your home is protected whether you're present or away — a particularly valuable feature for Lantana residents who travel or own seasonal properties in the area.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Lantana if I'm not right on the beach?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Lantana sits just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coast, which means wind speeds and storm surge risks affect the entire community — not just beachfront properties. Palm Beach County designates the area with a 170 mph design wind speed requirement, which applies to all homes regardless of their specific distance from the water. Neighborhoods like Pine Ridge and South Shores are fully exposed to hurricane-force conditions, and the area has been directly affected by eight named storms since 1990.

faqs.q: How much will impact windows cost for my Lantana home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: The cost of a full impact window replacement in Lantana typically ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 or more, depending on the number of windows, sizes, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing with $0 down and payments starting as low as $87 per month, making it accessible for most middle-income homeowners. We'll provide a detailed, no-obligation quote based on your specific home so you know exactly what to expect before committing.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my windows with impact windows in Lantana?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact window replacements in Lantana require a permit through the Town of Lantana Building Department. This includes submitting Florida Product Approval documentation for your specific window products, engineered drawings, and contractor licensing credentials. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from initial application through final inspection — so you don't have to navigate the paperwork alone. Unpermitted window work can create serious problems with insurance claims and home resale.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take for a typical Lantana home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Lantana with a standard number of window openings, the physical installation typically takes one to two days. However, the overall timeline from contract signing to completed installation includes the permit application and approval process, which can take two to four weeks depending on the Town of Lantana Building Department's current workload. Our team coordinates all scheduling and keeps you informed throughout the process. We aim to minimize disruption to your daily routine as much as possible.

faqs.q: Will impact windows really lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Lantana?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases they will. Florida law requires insurance carriers to provide wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who install qualifying hurricane protection products, including impact-rated windows. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your windows' ratings, and that report is submitted to your insurer to apply the credits. Lantana homeowners in Palm Beach County typically see premium reductions of 20–45% on the wind portion of their policy — often saving hundreds of dollars annually, which helps offset the cost of the upgrade over time.
